summary: This Islamic lecture discusses the rulings for women during menstruation and postpartum periods according to Islamic law. The speaker explains that postpartum bleeding lasts up to 40 days in Islamic legislation, after which a woman must perform ablution and pray even if bleeding continues. The lecture establishes that menstruation and postpartum have identical rulings in Islamic law except for three specific issues. A key focus is on fasting: women do not fast during menstruation or postpartum and must make up missed fasts later, but notably they are not required to make up missed prayers during these periods—a mercy from Allah due to the practical difficulty this would impose.
suggested_title: Islamic Rulings on Menstruation and Postpartum Periods: Fasting and Prayer
catchy_title: Why Women Don't Make Up Missed Prayers
